ogTitle: Pheasants Nest vs Phillip Bay: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Pheasants Nest and Phillip Bay.
heading: Pheasants Nest vs Phillip Bay — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Pheasants Nest, New South Wales and Phillip Bay, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Pheasants Nest has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Phillip Bay (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Pheasants Nest or Phillip Bay safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Pheasants Nest has a flood risk score of 56/100, while Phillip Bay scores 34/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Pheasants Nest scores 17/100 for bushfire risk. Phillip Bay scores 47/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Pheasants Nest or Phillip Bay?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Pheasants Nest has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.6% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
